response.justification: Wei Chen is a capable full-stack engineer with roughly 15 years of experience and genuine entrepreneurial spirit, but the profile falls far short of a Director-level Principal Engineer in AI Strategy. The role demands recognized AI expertise, cloud-platform strategy, and customer advisory experience at executive scale; Chen's AI exposure is limited to a single month at re:cinq starting April 2025 and consists of tooling work rather than product leadership. There is no evidence of advising strategic enterprise customers, shaping cloud product roadmaps, or building AI-powered platforms at any meaningful scale. The seniority gap alone — Staff SWE versus Director-level IC — makes this a poor fit.
strengths: ~15 years of continuous software engineering experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains
Demonstrated entrepreneurial initiative through founding GISCollective and building and maintaining an open-source geo-data platform used in production
Recent hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq signals genuine movement toward the AI domain
concerns: AI expertise is nascent and very recent (April 2025 onward), with no demonstrated recognition, published research, or AI-powered products built at scale
Current and historical titles (Senior Developer, Staff Engineer) indicate an execution-level seniority far below the Director-level IC this role requires
No evidence of strategic customer advisory work, cloud architecture at platform scale, or cross-organizational technical leadership — all core to the role's responsibilities
